other_observers: Cole Wild; I am not the original finder and identifier. This bird has been on a winter territory since at least December.
specific_location: Valco Ponds area of Pueblo Reservoir State Park
description: About 7 inches long, blackish gray head, breast, upperparts and tail. Grayish tips to wing coverts and gray edges to tertials. White belly. Black bill and feet.Behaviors: Perching on twig and rocks close to surface of Arkansas River, sallying to feed on insects on water surface.Call: sharp "pik"Plumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Nick Komar, Nick Komar, Nick Komar
